name: From Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga to Balcón de Europa of Nerja – circular via EuroVelo 8
name_original: De Alcazaba de Vélez-Málaga a Balcón de Europa de Nerja – circular por EuroVelo 8
description: Cycle 37.6 miles (60.6 km) from Vélez-Málaga's Alcazaba to Nerja's Balcón de Europa on this moderate EuroVelo 8 route.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Vélez-Málaga offer diverse terrain, from flat coastal roads to challenging mountain ascents. The region features picturesque coastal paths, including sections of the EuroVelo 8, and routes through tropical fruit groves. Inland, cyclists encounter hills and mountains of the Axarquía region, dotted with white villages. This varied landscape provides options for different cycling preferences and fitness levels. Best touring cycling routes around Vélez-Málaga The most popular touring cycling route is From Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga to Balcón de Europa of Nerja – circular via EuroVelo 8, a 37.6 miles (60.6 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route connects historical landmarks and offers coastal views as part of the EuroVelo 8 network. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Axarquía Raisin Route Cycle Loop, a difficult 25.3 miles (40.8 km) path. This route explores the agricultural landscapes of the Axarquía region, known for its fruit groves and traditional villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Lagos Beach – View of the Torre de Güi loop from Vélez-Málaga, a 24.9 miles (40.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Vélez-Málaga is defined by coastal roads, mountain trails, and routes through white villages and fruit groves. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to routes requiring significant elevation gains. The routes in Vélez-Málaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 480 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vélez-Málaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Vélez-Málaga?
A: Vélez-Málaga offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 110 routes mapped by the komoot community. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Vélez-Málaga?
A: Vélez-Málaga benefits from a mild climate with over 300 days of sunshine annually, making it suitable for cycling throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, while coastal routes remain pleasant even in winter.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when cycling around Vélez-Málaga?
A: The region boasts diverse terrain. You'll find flat, paved coastal roads, gentle climbs through tropical fruit groves, and challenging ascents into the mountains of the Axarquía region. This variety ensures options for all cycling levels and preferences.

Q: Are there options for different cycling abilities?
A: Yes, Vélez-Málaga offers routes for all abilities. There are 28 easy routes, 21 moderate routes, and 69 difficult routes, ensuring that both beginners and experienced cyclists can find suitable challenges.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks and natural beauty. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7114385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba Fortress&lt;/a&gt; in Vélez-Málaga, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381716" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cahorros Bajos del Río Higuerón&lt;/a&gt; gorge, or the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3889477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walkway Through the Río Algarrobo Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes often lead to viewpoints like the Balcón de Europa in Nerja.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vélez-Málaga?
A: The touring cycling routes in Vélez-Málaga are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 480 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from stunning coastal views to the charm of the white villages and the challenge of mountain climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling routes in Vélez-Málaga?
A: For families or those seeking an easier ride, the flat, paved coastal paths are ideal. The Río Seco near Caleta de Vélez, for example, offers an easy ride connecting urban areas with natural sections, providing views of the mountains without significant climbs.

Q: Can I find circular cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Vélez-Málaga are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976733409/from-alcazaba-of-velez-malaga-to-balcon-de-europa-of-nerja-circular-via-eurovelo-8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Alcazaba of Vélez-Málaga to Balcón de Europa of Nerja – circular via EuroVelo 8&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic coastal loop. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14599777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos Beach – View of the Torre de Güi loop from Vélez-Málaga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find routes with significant elevation gain for a challenge?
A: For a more demanding experience, explore the mountain roads of the Axarquía region. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e981379239/axarquia-raisin-route-cycle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Axarquía Raisin Route Cycle Loop&lt;/a&gt; involve considerable climbing, offering rewarding descents and panoramic views. The Montes de Málaga also provide a network of challenging trails.

Q: Are there cycling routes that pass through the famous 'white villages'?
A: Absolutely. The Axarquía region is renowned for its 'white villages' (Pueblos Blancos). Routes like the Ruta de Mudejar weave through picturesque villages such as Canillas de Aceituno, Sedella, and Archez, showcasing traditional architecture amidst vineyards and local produce shops.

Q: What are some notable natural features I might encounter on a ride?
A: Beyond the coastline, you'll find lush tropical fruit groves, especially north of Vélez-Málaga, with mangos, papayas, and avocados. The scenic Lake Viñuela offers a rewarding loop, and the Barranco de Maro gorge impresses with its spectacular landscape and sea views.

Q: Is public transport accessible for cyclists in Vélez-Málaga?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, Vélez-Málaga and its surrounding towns are generally well-connected by local bus services. It's advisable to check with individual operators regarding bike transport policies, especially for longer journeys or during peak times.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, cycling through the villages and along the coast provides ample opportunities to stop. You'll find local bodegas, shops selling regional produce, and beachside 'chiringuitos' offering tapas and drinks with Mediterranean views, perfect for a break.
tips: At the highest point of the city of Vélez-Málaga is Alcazaba de Vélez - Málaga. It is a fortress of Arab origin, built in the 9th century. From this point we can enjoy the most emblematic view of the city.
This section offers choice between the quiet rural road and the unpaved but even quieter farm roads and trails in parallel. Either way the views of the Sierras towering to the East are stunning
Look up the Río Seco valley to the snow-capped mountains.
Lagos beach consists of a mixture of sand and pebbles. Its length and narrow shape make it ideal for those who prefer quiet and unspoiled beaches.
Nice view up to the old coastal watchtower. It can be accessed a bit to the east via a steep road leading to a footpath

This 15th century conical watchtower stands at a height of about 40 metres and is clearly visible from the road. The tower is located on private, fenced, inaccessible land, so it can only be viewed from below.

A quieter and lesser-known stretch of coast around Nerja. This region is characterized by natural coves, often located away from the main beaches and less frequented. The beach consists of pebbles and sand, surrounded by rocks. The nearby river (although usually dry) shapes the surrounding area and provides a beautiful backdrop.

The now famous called Balcón de Europa de Nerja was formerly the Paseo la Bateria, a defensive fort from which enemies that could arrive from North Africa were sighted, to prevent attacks and assaults by pirate ships.

The aqueduct Águila (translated as the eagle) in Nerja is a very large aqueduct and immediately catches the eye. 37 arches made of brick span four floors. In total, the aqueduct is around forty meters high. The water supply comes from an open canal on the top floor.

The entire building is reminiscent of the Mudejar style typical of Andalusia, especially on its northern side. The Mudéjares were assimilated Muslims who were allowed to remain in Spain after Arab rule was pushed back from the 11th to 15th centuries.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for this circular route is approximately 3 hours and 45 minutes. This is for the 60.6 km distance with over 420 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: How challenging is the cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: This route is considered moderate in difficulty, but it features a 'subi-baja' (up-and-down) profile due to the mountains meeting the sea in this region. Cyclists should expect frequent ascents and descents, providing a good workout.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this EuroVelo 8 section?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, featuring a mix of surfaces. You can expect asphalt, paved sections, unpaved paths, and potentially cobblestones. The route utilizes bike paths, secondary roads, and some sections of busier roads, sometimes moving inland.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and scenic views along the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects the historic Alcazaba Fortress in Vélez-Málaga with the iconic Balcony of Europe in Nerja. You'll experience stunning Mediterranean coastal views, picturesque inland landscapes, and pass by features like Camino de Torrox - Sierras view, Río Seco, and Lagos Beach. Don't miss the Eagle Aqueduct (Acueducto del Águila).

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa del Sol generally offers pleasant cycling weather for much of the year. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) are ideal, as temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially during midday.

faqs.question: Where can I start and park for this route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Vélez-Málaga, you can begin near the Alcazaba Fortress. Parking options would typically be available in Vélez-Málaga town center or nearby public parking areas, though specific trailhead parking isn't detailed. Consider public transport options to Vélez-Málaga if you prefer not to drive.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily uses roads and paths, the suitability for dogs depends on their fitness level and comfort with cycling alongside. Always ensure dogs are on a leash and consider the varied terrain and potential traffic. It's best to check local regulations for specific sections, especially around beaches or protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for cycling along the EuroVelo 8 or public roads and paths in this region. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Alcazaba or Balcón de Europa, they may have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects Vélez-Málaga and Nerja, both towns offer numerous cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. You'll also find smaller villages and coastal towns along the way with places to stop for refreshments and meals.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer:
Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of

La Senda Litoral (41%)

,

Vélez-Málaga - Torrox (23%)

, and

Torrox - Nerja (16%)

. You'll also encounter smaller segments of routes like

Ruta Roja (6%)

and

Torrox - Torrox Costa (1%)

.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty, significant distance (60.6 km), and 'subi-baja' profile with challenging climbs, this route might be too demanding for young children or absolute cycling beginners. Families with older, experienced cyclists or beginners looking for a challenge could consider it, but be prepared for the effort involved.
